messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0010",
  "title": "少数巨头的生态守卫战 vs 庞大长尾的残酷生存战",
  "page_index": 5,
  "page_range": "5"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0011",
    "title": "2023年",
    "page_range": "5",
    "summary": "This section provides 2023 quantitative metrics on mobile game revenue distribution by ranking tier. It answers questions regarding: the exact revenue market share percentages for the top 10 games (43.14%), ranks 11–50 (26.62%), ranks 51–200 (19.45%), and all other mobile games (10.79%); the level of market concentration within the 2023 mobile gaming sector; and the statistical breakdown of revenue across ranked categories. The content consists solely of percentage-based metrics and statistical evidence, with no definitions, procedures, tables, figures, or qualitative claims provided."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0012",
    "title": "2024年",
    "page_range": "5",
    "summary": "This section provides quantitative metrics on the 2024 mobile game market revenue distribution by ranking tier. It can answer retrieval questions regarding:\n- **Date:** 2024\n- **Topics & Entities:** Mobile game revenue market share across four defined ranking tiers (1-10, 11-50, 51-200, and Others)\n- **Metrics & Evidence Types:** Direct statistical percentages for revenue scale proportions: 43.23% (Top 10), 28.68% (11-50), 18.62% (51-200), and 9.47% (Others)\n- **Claims:** Market revenue is heavily concentrated in the top tier, with progressively smaller shares held by mid-tier, lower-tier, and unranked titles.\n- **Definitions, Procedures, Tables, Figures:** None included; data is presented as a standalone statistical list.\nSuitable for queries on 2024 mobile gaming market concentration, tier-specific revenue breakdowns, and industry share metrics."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0013",
    "title": "2025年",
    "page_range": "5-1",
    "summary": "This section answers retrieval queries regarding 2025 mobile game market revenue distribution by ranking tier.\n- **Topics/Entities:** Mobile game revenue concentration, ranking brackets (1-10, 11-50, 51-200, others), 点点数据 (Diandian Data).\n- **Dates:** 2025 (primary data year); references a three-year comparative trend period.\n- **Metrics:** 1-10 rank: 44.01%; 11-50 rank: 27.20%; 51-200 rank: 19.08%; Others: 9.71%.\n- **Figures/Visuals:** Color-coded bar chart (blue: 1-10, cyan: 11-50, green: 51-200, orange: others) with representative game icons.\n- **Claims/Trends:** Top 10 revenue share fluctuates slightly but remains around 43% over three years; mid-tier (11-50, 51-200) shares exhibit more pronounced changes; other games' share is consistently low and stable.\n- **Evidence Type:** Independent industry research data and proprietary charting by 点点数据.\n- **Supported Queries:** What is the 2025 mobile game revenue share breakdown by ranking tier? How concentrated is the mobile game market among top-ranked titles versus mid/long-tail games? What are the three-year trends in mobile game revenue distribution across ranking brackets? What data source and visual encoding are used for this market analysis?"
  }
]

message.content: This section analyzes mobile game market revenue distribution and concentration across 2023, 2024, and 2025, contrasting the dominance of top-tier titles with the performance of mid-tier and long-tail games. It provides exact percentage metrics for four ranking brackets (1–10, 11–50, 51–200, and Others) for each year: 2023 (43.14%, 26.62%, 19.45%, 10.79%), 2024 (43.23%, 28.68%, 18.62%, 9.47%), and 2025 (44.01%, 27.20%, 19.08%, 9.71%). The content supports retrieval queries on annual revenue breakdowns, market concentration levels, and three-year tier performance trends. Key claims indicate that revenue is heavily concentrated in the top 10, with this tier's share remaining stable around 43% over the period, while mid-tier shares exhibit more fluctuation and the long-tail segment stays consistently low. The 2025 subsection includes a color-coded bar chart visualizing the tiers and cites 点点数据 (Diandian Data) as the evidence source, relying on independent industry research data and proprietary statistical charting. No definitions, procedures, or tables are included.

response_text: This section analyzes mobile game market revenue distribution and concentration across 2023, 2024, and 2025, contrasting the dominance of top-tier titles with the performance of mid-tier and long-tail games. It provides exact percentage metrics for four ranking brackets (1–10, 11–50, 51–200, and Others) for each year: 2023 (43.14%, 26.62%, 19.45%, 10.79%), 2024 (43.23%, 28.68%, 18.62%, 9.47%), and 2025 (44.01%, 27.20%, 19.08%, 9.71%). The content supports retrieval queries on annual revenue breakdowns, market concentration levels, and three-year tier performance trends. Key claims indicate that revenue is heavily concentrated in the top 10, with this tier's share remaining stable around 43% over the period, while mid-tier shares exhibit more fluctuation and the long-tail segment stays consistently low. The 2025 subsection includes a color-coded bar chart visualizing the tiers and cites 点点数据 (Diandian Data) as the evidence source, relying on independent industry research data and proprietary statistical charting. No definitions, procedures, or tables are included.
